title: US denies Iranian claim of agreeing to unfreeze assets amid talks
content: The White House has denied reports of an agreement to release Iran's frozen $6 billion in foreign banks, a claim made by a senior Iranian source amid ongoing ceasefire negotiations.

title: 'No deal’ if ‘Israel first’: Iran draws red line amid US talks
content: High-level Iranian and US delegations are in Islamabad for critical talks aimed at stabilizing a fragile ceasefire. Iran's Vice President stated a deal hinges on meeting 'America First' representatives, warning against 'Israel First' engagement. Prime Minister Sharif called the negotiations 'make or break,' with a 15-day window for a diplomatic resolution or renewed conflict.

title: Two years after Google CEO Sundar Pichai’s ‘Code Red’, Satya Nadella orders AI overhaul
content: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella has initiated a 'Copilot code red' to enhance AI performance and user experience, aiming to boost investor confidence amidst intense competition.  The company is allocating significant cloud capacity to Copilot development, with analysts suggesting Azure can still exceed expectations due to growing demand and pricing.

title: Sunrisers Hyderabad create IPL history, become first team to ...
content: Sunrisers Hyderabad's opening pair, Abhishek Sharma and Travis Head, unleashed an unprecedented Powerplay assault against Punjab Kings, smashing 105 runs without loss. Abhishek recorded a blistering half-century in 18 balls and equaled the record for most sixes in a Powerplay with seven, highlighting SRH's dominance.

title: Virat Kohli's 'naagin dance' thrills crowd; fans respond in style - WATCH
content: Rajasthan Royals secured a six-wicket victory over Royal Challengers Bengaluru in Guwahati. Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's explosive 78 off 26 balls and Dhruv Jurel's unbeaten 81 off 43 deliveries were instrumental. Earlier, Rajat Patidar's 63 had guided RCB to 201. Virat Kohli's pre-match dance entertained the crowd. Rajasthan remains unbeaten.

title: Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff has a message for engineers as AI takes over their jobs
content: Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff says AI coding agents have made the company's 15,000 engineers over 30% more productive—and that's exactly why Salesforce didn't hire a single new engineer in fiscal year 2026. Speaking on The Future Live, Benioff said engineers are shifting from writing code to supervising AI agents. He also revealed AI now handles up to 50% of Salesforce's total workload, as the company doubles down on what Benioff calls the "digital labor revolution."

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' box office collection: Ranveer Singh starrer inches towards Rs 1100cr
content: The buzz around Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' shows no signs of waning, as it approaches the astounding mark of Rs 1100 crore within just 24 days. This espionage thriller has set a new benchmark in the industry, being the first Bollywood film to achieve the Rs 1000 crore milestone in India. Read the full article to know more in detail.

title: US-Iran war: At nearly $700bn, how India’s forex are strong armour against global crises
content: Fundamentally, foreign exchange reserves tell us about a country’s ability to service its external debt. The more robust the cover, the better a country’s position of economic strength. So how strong is India placed in terms of foreign exchange reserves?

title: ‘Gandhi mein aag lag rahi hai bhai’: Firefighters recall cash discovery at Justice Verma's house
content: A fire at Justice Yashwant Varma's Delhi residence revealed partially burnt high-denomination currency notes, sparking a high-level judicial and governmental probe. A fireman's remark about Gandhi on the notes, captured on video, highlighted the incident's gravity. The discovery led to swift action, with the Delhi Police Commissioner briefing the Chief Justice and a report sent to the Union Home Ministry.

title: Google AI CEO Demis Hassabis says ChatGPT did something in 2022 that can't be undone
content: Demis Hassabis, CEO of Google DeepMind, says ChatGPT's viral 2022 launch triggered a "ferocious commercial pressure race" that nobody—including OpenAI—was prepared for. Speaking to Cleo Abram, he said every major lab had equivalent systems at the time; OpenAI just shipped first. Hassabis had wanted AI kept in the lab longer, focused on scientific breakthroughs. Now he's racing alongside everyone else—and watching the guardrails get harder to build.

title: Parkinson’s disease: The brain disorder that often begins silently years before tremors appear
content: Mr. Bipin's subtle tremors and slowed movements foreshadowed a Parkinson's diagnosis, a progressive neurological disorder affecting millions. Beyond visible motor symptoms like tremors and stiffness, Parkinson's also presents with often-missed non-motor signs such as sleep disturbances and mood changes, highlighting the need for early recognition and comprehensive support.

title: Sindarov, Gukesh's likely challenger, cracks code for sponsorship woes in chess
content: Uzbekistan's Javokhir Sindarov is on the cusp of winning the FIDE Candidates 2026, poised to challenge World Champion D Gukesh. His rapid play and impressive unbeaten streak have captivated the chess world. Managed by Tom Guilbaud, Sindarov's career has transformed from obscurity to a top contender, aided by strategic social media promotion and newfound sponsorships.

title: Father 'sprayed' perfume: Teacher’s skeletal remains found in Meerut home 5 months after death
content: Skeletal remains of a teacher, Priyanka Biswas, were discovered in her Meerut home five months after her death. Her father allegedly kept the body, spraying perfume to mask the odor, until relatives alerted authorities. Police are investigating the cause of death and the reason for the delayed cremation.

title: In MP village, swear words invite Rs 500 fine
content: In Madhya Pradesh's Burhanpur district, Borsar village has implemented a unique rule where swearing incurs a Rs 500 fine or an hour of street sweeping. This initiative, driven by local leaders and social workers, aims to curb verbal abuse and foster civility.

title: Pakistan-linked handler targeting youth, two men arrested in Bijnor tell police
content: Two men arrested in Bijnor, Uttar Pradesh, revealed a Pakistan-linked handler is building a network in India. The handler, based in Saudi Arabia, used social media platforms like Instagram. He allegedly spread inflammatory content and incited youngsters against Hindus. Police are searching for the handler and other associates involved in anti-national activities.

title: Justice Varma saves himself from ignoble first
content: Former Allahabad High Court judge Justice Yashwant Varma resigned, avoiding parliamentary removal proceedings. This follows a history of judges facing removal motions, with some resigning and others escaping proceedings. The constitutional process for judge removal involves parliamentary address based on proved misbehaviour or incapacity.

title: What if PM Modi still loses?: Kejriwal alleges cutting of votes ahead of Bengal polls
content: Arvind Kejriwal has accused Prime Minister Modi of institutional takeover and manipulating votes ahead of the West Bengal elections. This comes as the state braces for polls amidst sharp exchanges between the TMC and BJP over voter lists and electoral preparedness. The BJP, meanwhile, has promised Uniform Civil Code and action against infiltration if elected.

title: ‘Victims sexually harassed, forced to eat beef’: What we know about Nashik IT firm case
content: A special investigation team is probing multiple sexual harassment allegations at a Nashik IT firm, leading to seven arrests, including an HR official.  Victims report mental and sexual harassment, with claims of forced religious practices and blackmail.  The probe is examining potential wider patterns and compliance with the PoSH Act.

title: West Bengal polls: PM Modi vows to release 'white paper' on alleged TMC corruption
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi intensified the BJP's campaign in West Bengal, focusing on infiltration, citizenship under CAA, and alleged TMC corruption. He promised expedited citizenship for refugees and warned infiltrators to leave, while also highlighting alleged fear and corruption under the TMC government.

title: How often should you get a haircut for healthy hair growth?
content: Haircuts do not make hair grow faster, but they are essential for healthy, thick-looking hair. Regular trims prevent split ends and breakage, helping you retain length. For most people, a trim every 8 to 12 weeks is ideal. Focusing on scalp health, nutrition, and gentle handling also promotes growth. Wise trims save hair from damage.

title: Sooryavanshi, boy wonder, scripts IPL history; only batter with 300+ strike rate to...
content: With two scores of fifty or more at a strike rate of 300 or above, Sooryavanshi has become the only player in IPL history to record multiple half-centuries at that strike rate after the completion of innings (whether out or not out).

title: Ayush Shetty stuns World No. 1, first Indian man in Asian Championships single's final in 61 years
content: India's Ayush Shetty achieved a career-defining victory at the Badminton Asia Championships, defeating top seed and defending champion Kunlavut Vitidsarn in a thrilling three-game match. This remarkable comeback win secures Shetty at least a silver medal, making him the first Indian men's singles player to reach the final since Dinesh Khanna in 1965.

title: ‘I know my limits’: Stalin plays down national role, projects big win in upcoming polls
content: Tamil Nadu CM M.K. Stalin downplayed national ambitions, stating he knows his limits, while confidently predicting a DMK-led alliance sweep of over 200 seats in the upcoming Assembly elections. He highlighted welfare schemes and attacked rivals AIADMK and BJP, accusing the former of lacking ideology and being controlled by Delhi.

title: From queues to QR codes: How UPI transformed India’s digital payments
content: India's financial landscape has been revolutionized by UPI, the nation's digital payments centerpiece. Launched in 2016, it has become the world's largest real-time payment system by volume, processing billions of transactions monthly. UPI has significantly expanded financial inclusion, bringing millions into the digital economy and setting a global benchmark for inclusive financial innovation.

title: 'Will only proceed with US support': UK pulls out of Chagos deal after Trump's criticism
content: Britain's proposed Chagos Islands deal with Mauritius has stalled due to US approval uncertainties, particularly Donald Trump's opposition. The agreement, which would transfer sovereignty while leasing Diego Garcia, faces legislative hurdles before Parliament dissolves. Officials emphasize the deal's dependence on US backing for the strategic base's security, leaving its future uncertain amid ongoing negotiations.

title: Congress slams PM Modi over US-Iran talks in Pakistan
content: Congress leader Jairam Ramesh criticizes PM Modi's foreign policy after the US chose Pakistan to mediate talks with Iran. Ramesh questions how Pakistan secured this role despite its terror links and India's diplomatic efforts, highlighting perceived failures in leveraging ties with the US and BRICS+.

title: 'Kal se khel sakte hain?': What Sooryavanshi asked doctors after being hit on helmet
content: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, at 15, is taking the world’s best bowlers to the cleaners. After taking on Mumbai Indians' Jasprit Bumrah a few days ago, he tore apart Royal Challengers Bengaluru seamers Josh Hazlewood and Bhuvneshwar Kumar as well. Sooryavanshi does not bother with reputations or resumes. The youngster bats with rare clarity and shows intent from ball one, and his personal side-arm thrower back in Tajpur, Samastipur, is not surprised at all.

title: Who is Amit Kshatriya? Indian-American Nasa official behind Moon mission
content: Indian-American space scientist Amit Kshatriya holds a senior leadership role at NASA, contributing to the agency's renewed human lunar exploration. Following the successful Artemis II mission, a crewed lunar flyby, Kshatriya emphasized the significant work ahead for future deep space endeavors.

title: BJP, EC tried to invalidate my Bhabanipur candidature: Mamata at Keshiyari rally
content: West Bengal CM Mamata Banerjee accused the BJP of attempting to invalidate her candidature from Bhabanipur through false cases with the Election Commission's help. She also alleged the BJP manipulated electoral rolls, leading to the deletion of millions of voters, and claimed they planned to tamper with EVMs to influence poll results.

title: No renovation needed: 5 easy colour swaps for fresh, airy-feel spring home decor
content: Spring brings a desire for lighter homes. Art therapists share simple colour swaps for every room. These changes, from living room throws to kitchen linens, require no major redecorating. Small accessory updates can instantly lift the mood. Experts suggest pastel shades and fresh tones to welcome the season. These easy updates make spaces feel airy and renewed.

title: Weight-loss injections deliver in real life: 4 in 10 Indians shed 10% body weight
content: New weight-loss injections are proving effective for Indian patients, with over 40% shedding at least 10% of their body weight in a recent Delhi hospital study.  While results vary, especially for those with diabetes, the therapies offer significant health benefits.  Experts emphasize these are not quick fixes but valuable tools when combined with lifestyle changes for sustained weight reduction.

title: US-Iran ceasefire: JD Vance lands in Islamabad to lead talks amid fragile truce - watch
content: US Vice President JD Vance is in Islamabad for critical talks with Iran, aiming to solidify a fragile ceasefire and pursue peace in the Middle East. The negotiations, brokered by Pakistan, face significant hurdles, including Iran's preconditions for a Lebanon ceasefire and asset release. Tensions remain high amid regional violence and economic disruption.

title: Watch: PM Modi, Rahul engage in candid talks during Jyotiba Phule tribute ceremony
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Leader of Opposition Rahul Gandhi shared a brief, candid conversation at a Parliament tribute ceremony for Mahatma Jyotiba Phule. The interaction drew attention given their history of sharp political exchanges and public criticisms of each other. Both leaders separately paid homage to the social reformer's legacy.

title: 10 dead in Mathura boat tragedy: From bhajans to screams of terror in a split second; watch
content: A Vrindavan boat tragedy has left at least 10 devotees from Ludhiana dead, with five still missing after their boat capsized in the Yamuna. Survivors recount a terrifying ordeal, singing bhajans moments before the accident. Rescue operations are ongoing, and helplines have been established to assist affected families.

title: TMC, BJP supporters clash during campaign in West Bengal’s Hooghly; FIR lodged
content: Trinamool Congress and BJP supporters clashed during an election campaign in West Bengal's Hooghly district, resulting in minor injuries to four BJP workers. The incident occurred as campaigning intensifies for the 2026 Assembly elections, with both parties trading sharp political attacks and releasing manifestos.

title: McIlroy is all alone through 36 holes; can anyone catch him?
content: One year after winning his first Masters, McIlroy has a record 6-shot lead and has 36 more holes to add.

title: Red-hot Rory sets Masters record with 6-shot lead
content: Rory McIlroy closed with six birdies over his last seven holes to finish at 7-under 65 and push his lead to six shots on Friday, the largest at the Masters after 36 holes.

title: SGA, Jokic among at least 168 out NBA-wide Fri.
content: Around the NBA, no fewer than 168 players were held out of Friday's games, including reigning MVP Shai Gilgeous-Alexander, three-time MVP Nikola Jokic and 14 members of the Memphis Grizzlies.

title: Dodgers' Muncy caps 3-HR night with walk-off shot
content: Max Muncy hit three home runs, including a game-winning, two-out solo blast in the ninth inning, to rally the Dodgers past the Rangers 8-7 on Friday night.

title: Wembanyama checks another 'box,' hits 65 games
content: Victor Wembanyama reached the 65-game threshold for awards eligibility on Friday with a 40-point outing that included 13 rebounds in a 139-120 win over the Dallas Mavericks.

title: Celtics tie NBA record with 29 3s, clinch 2-seed
content: The Celtics tied the franchise and NBA record with 29 3s on their way to clinching the Eastern Conference's No. 2 playoff seed in a blowout win over the Pelicans on Friday night.

title: Sources: Griner finalizing 7-figure deal to join Sun
content: Two-time WNBA scoring champion Brittney Griner is finalizing a seven-figure deal to join the Connecticut Sun, sources confirmed to ESPN's Alexa Philippou on Friday.

title: Redick: 'Everybody' wants to face hobbled Lakers
content: Lakers coach JJ Redick believes other West teams that still have their seeding to be determined prefer L.A. as their first-round opponent in the playoffs.

title: Sources: NYT investigating reporter's Vrabel ties
content: The Athletic is reinvestigating the coverage of NFL reporter Dianna Russini and the nature of her relationship with Patriots head coach Mike Vrabel amid questions raised about the two this week, sources told ESPN on Friday.

title: 🏈 NFL draft rankings: Miller's top 482 players
content: Matt Miller has finalized his rankings for the 2026 draft, listing top players and providing grades for each one.

title: NBA playoff intel: Early buzz on the Lakers, Celtics, Cavs, Pistons Spurs and Warriors
content: Are the Lakers toast? Is the Spurs' 3-point prowess real? We canvass the league on six storylines that could define the postseason.

title: King's gambit: Why NBA players are turning to chess
content: NBA players are spending more time than ever working on the boards -- but we're not talking rebounds.

title: 'It's the new frontier': How AI is pushing NFL draft prep to 'a different level'
content: When a prospect skips NFL combine workouts, teams can use artificial intelligence to project his measurables.

title: Free agency grades: Sizing up the Eagles' Dontayvion Wicks trade and more
content: We're handing out report cards on big moves, including the Raiders' Kirk Cousins deal and the Eagles' Dontayvion Wicks trade.

title: UCLA and its six seniors are eyeing a WNBA draft takeover
content: Can UCLA make history and have all six seniors -- who scored all of the team's points at the Final Four -- drafted in the WNBA?

title: WNBA draft preview: The case for every potential No. 1 pick
content: Will Lauren Betts, Awa Fam, Azzi Fudd or Olivia Miles go No. 1, and which transfers boosted their draft stock?

title: WNBA free agency predictions, biggest questions and more
content: Who are the biggest names who might be on the move, and how will the condensed offseason impact free agency?

title: From Ivy League catcher to Yankees' cleanup hitter: The under-the-radar rise of Ben Rice
content: Surrounded by All-Stars, a 27-year-old in just his second full season is making his mark in the Bronx.

title: MLB Power Rankings: Brewers, Pirates rise as Red Sox fall in Week 2
content: Teams with championship hopes fall down our list while unexpected contenders have made surprise starts.

title: Free agency turns 50: Ranking the best -- and worst -- signings in MLB history
content: The first free agent contract ever was signed 50 years ago today. We rate the 8,340 deals of the past half-century.

title: A big summer awaits Manchester United, where transfers take priority
content: Manchester United are making the final push to end the season on a high note, before an important summer of recruitment for players and coach alike.

title: Is failing to qualify for the CL actually better for Premier League teams?
content: The UEFA Champions League is the pinnacle of club soccer, but maybe for PL teams, it's just an extra headache?

title: From Cyndi Lauper to 'The Man': The evolution of women's wrestling at WrestleMania
content: Dive into how women's professional wrestling has evolved on WWE's biggest stage over the past 40 years.

title: Rankings: Top 100 players for 2026
content: Plan for the 2026 Fantasy Women's Basketball season with the top 100 rankings, including player position ranks.

title: Baldwin best catcher? Alcantara is back? Cam Smith a top-50 bat? Don't be surprised
content: Eric Karabell looks into the crystal ball to tell fantasy baseball managers what might come to pass in the not-too-distant future.

title: Comparing the best 2026 recruits to previous classes
content: How good are next year's freshmen? We pit them against the best of 2024 (Sarah Strong) and 2025 (Jazzy Davidson).

title: Max Verstappen's inner circle is unravelling -- and so are Red Bull's hopes of keeping him
content: The departure of Max Verstappen's longtime race engineer Gianpiero Lambiase will add fuel to the inferno that is building over the four-time world champion's immediate F1 future, writes ESPN's Nate Saunders.

title: Transfer rumors, news: Arsenal eye summer move for Newcastle's Gordon
content: Arsenal are considering a summer move for Newcastle United's Anthony Gordon. Transfer Talk has the latest.

title: LPG tanker Jag Vikram crosses Strait of Hormuz; first India-flagged vessel to transit after Iran-U.S. ceasefire
content: Owned by Mumbai-based Great Eastern Shipping Company, Jag Vikram is carrying around 20,000 tonnes of LPG

title: Uttar Pradesh ATS arrests Muslim cleric for sharing inflammatory posts on social media
content: ATS said that probe revealed the arrested cleric was posting messages and sharing content via social media to incite people by opposing CAA, NRC, UGC, SIR, the Ram Temple verdict, and actions taken during the “I Love Mohammed” protests

title: Amaidhipadai re-release: a political film that still hits hard
content: Amaidhipadai threw a light on how vile politics at the highest level could be, and was laced with commercial elements

title: ‘Jana Nayagan’ leak tests new legal penalties, torrent downloads under scanner
content: As the film’s producers rush to contain the fallout of the Vijay-starrer’s leak, their anti-piracy partner is attempting to stem distribution, while preserving torrent traffic logs

title: Jana Nayagan: T.N. police launch probe into leak of TVK leader Vijay’s film
content: The police action follows a complaint by R. Udayakumar, production controller of KVN Productions, who sought immediate intervention against the illegal leak, unlawful downloading, transmission, and circulation of the film, which is yet to be released in theatres

title: Assembly Elections LIVE: BJP government in West Bengal will speed up CAA implementation, says PM Modi at rally in Katwa
content: "I want to tell the Matua and Namasudra refugee families that they are under the protection of the country's Constitution," says PM Modi at a rally

title: Mercury crosses 40°C mark in Kerala, pushing ‘feels like’ temperatures distressingly high
content: Palakkad recorded the highest temperature of the season on April 10, 2026 at 40.1°C, , which was 3.4°C higher than the long-period average for the region. Punalur in Kollam recorded the second-highest temperature at 39.8°C, around 4.5°C above the average seasonal temperature.

title: Bharat cannot become $10 trillion economy by capital or policy alone: CJI Surya Kant
content: The CJI underscored the need for predictability, specialisation, and a culture of good faith in commercial law to help the country achieve this goal

title: Seven injured in clash in Karur; 19 DMK and AIADMK cadres booked
content: The clash was sparked by the distribution of pamphlets related to a property ownership dispute between a section of residents of Vennaimalai and the HR&CE Department

title: Pressure on Meghalaya council to prepare tribal-only poll rolls
content: The elections to the Garo Hills Autonomous District Council, scheduled for April 10, were postponed following violence related to nominations filed by non-tribal candidates, mostly Bengali-speaking Muslims

title: Mathura boat tragedy: Death toll rises to 11; vessel operator held
content: The accident occurred near Kesi Ghat when the boat carrying over two dozen tourists moved into deeper waters and hit a floating pontoon

title: NIA to take over terror links case from NTR Police
content: The West Zone police so far arrested 12 persons, including a woman and a minor, who allegedly had links with ISIS and AQIS leaders

title: Prakasam Collector spends night at Pothavaram village in Darsi
content: The District Collector listened patiently to every issue raised by the villagers and directed officials to resolve the issues at the earliest

title: K. Manivasan to be Tamil Nadu Home Secretary
content: Incumbent Dheeraj Kumar is to be tranferred out and not to be appointed to any election-related post until the completion of the polls, the ECI said in its communication

title: Women's reservation law amendment should not be used as political tool: Shashi Tharoor
content: Tharoor also asserted that Congress is committed to a one-third reservation for women, but the same should be inclusive and fair

title: In Chettinad, the holiday comes with homework
content: At the Kalai festival of Art and Architecture, curated by The Lotus Palace, historic mansions are turning into spaces for learning, drawing travellers seeking a deeper engagement with heritage, design and culture

title: Rediscovering Vaanam: How a contemporary festival in Tamil Nadu celebrates Dalit History Month
content: Four editions in, Neelam’s annual Dalit History Month celebration Vaanam, gets a new lease of life, with a competition category in their film festival, some dramatics, a literary festival, and an art exhibit

title: KHB invites bids for 80,000-seat stadium in Anekal amid push for larger venue
content: The project, estimated at ₹943.46 crore, will be executed on an Engineering, Procurement and Construction (EPC Mode-1) turnkey basis through a two-bid system via the Karnataka Public Procurement Portal, the tender notification said.

title: Centre nudges States to view farm solarisation as a route to wiping off ₹2.4 lakh crore subsidy bill
content: The ALMM — the Approved List of Models and Manufacturers — is the Centre’s primary tool for building a domestic solar manufacturing base

title: 10 killed as overcrowded boat capsizes in Yamuna
content: The boat was ferrying around 30 passengers between Vrindavan and nearby areas when it reportedly collided with an iron pontoon bridge built across the river

title: Nitish Kumar expected to resign as Bihar CM on April 13
content: Ruling JD(U) MP from Jhanjharpur constituency said that it has almost become clear that the next Chief Minister of Bihar would be from BJP; Nitish Kumar took oath as a member of Rajya Sabha on April 10, marking an end to his two-decade stint in office and paving the way for a new CM.

title: Repolling underway at booth of Karimganj North Assembly seat in Assam
content: "There were reports of some issues on April 9, the polling day for the Assam Assembly elections, and hence, the repolling was ordered,” the official said.

title: Uttar Pradesh boat tragedy: Punjab town mourns deaths
content: A boat carrying tourists, primarily from Punjab, capsized in the river in the holy town of Uttar Pradesh on Friday

title: Vijay’s ‘Jana Nayagan’ leaked online: Rajinikanth, Kamal Haasan, Chiranjeevi slam piracy
content: Reacting to the news of Tamil superstar Vijay’s ‘Jana Nayagan’ being leaked online, celebrities such as Rajinikanth, Kamal Haasan, Chiranjeevi, Vijay Deverakonda, and Suriya took to social media to slam piracy

title: Fire at Hyderabad’s Chintal Basti apartment, 17 residents evacuated safely
content: According to officials, the fire originated on the ground floor in the electrical meter area, and is suspected to have been caused by an electrical fault

title: Relief for Bengaluru commuters as Silk Board flyover set to open fully, but new bottleneck looms
content: The Bengaluru Traffic Police has revealed a likely bottleneck at Aravind Junction, and planned interventions such as increasing signal pass time and closing two left turns after the down ramp during peak hours.

title: Energy, defence and trade on agenda as Misri heads to Europe amid West Asia crisis
content: The West Asia crisis and its impact on energy security are expected to figure prominently in Mr. Misri’s talks with the interlocutors in Paris and Berlin

title: Vijay’s ‘Jana Nayagan’ controversy explained: From legal battles to piracy chaos
content: Here’s a comprehensive look into the legal battle behind actor-politician Vijay’s swansong film ‘Jana Nayagan’. The film, directed by H Vinoth, fell into a fresh controversy when it was leaked online recently

title: From hiding to hope: Bastar and its surrendered Maoists
content: The CAPF camp drive, security grid expansion, wipeout of leadership, erasure of corridors, and other reasons have resulted in Maoists surrendering in Chhattisgarh’s Bastar. Vijaita Singh reports on how the Maoist insurgency has fallen over the past two years, especially since the killing of its top leader Basavaraju last year, followed by the death of nine central committee members in encounters.

title: India, Bangladesh share ‘warm and historic ties’: MEA
content: Bangladesh Foreign Minister Khalilur Rahman arrives in India, ending 17-month-long cold phase in bilateral ties between the neighbouring countries

title: Welfare measures for the marginalised take centre stage in Bengal’s Jhargram
content: SCs/STs make up half the population of Jhargram, giving them a decisive say in the poll verdict; TMC, BJP clash over development plans, UCC implementation in a district emerging from the long shadow of Maoist extremism

title: Andhra Pradesh’s Socio-Economic Survey missing in recent Budget Session; efforts underway
content: Principal Secretary (Planning) Peeyush Kumar said the delay was due to unspecified reasons, but he indicated that efforts are underway to have it released next week and subsequently tabled

title: In Assam, first evicted, now erased
content: After a revision of electoral rolls in Assam, many people, mostly Bengali-speaking Muslims evicted from government land, lost their voting rights and now hold voter IDs deemed invalid. The fear of being made non-citizens is strongest at Kachutali village, about 30 km east of Guwahati, where more than 2,000 voters could not be a part of the democratic process of electing a new government, reports Rahul Karmakar

title: ED team visits residence of ex-Bengal Education Minister Partha Chatterjee over school jobs scam
content: The team conducted a search at his residence and questioned him, an ED official said, adding that Mr. Chatterjee did not attend an earlier summons after his release on bail

title: DMK demands reinstatement of N. Muruganandam as Tamil Nadu Chief Secretary
content: The party also strongly objected to the appointment of Sandeep Mittal as DGP (Armed Police and Vigilance and Anti-Corruption), alleging that Mr. Mittal was a supporter of the BJP and the RSS

title: T.N. Assembly election: Concerned over stalled growth, Ambattur constituency residents demand GH, sewer network, wider roads
content: Residents want an additional platform on the southern side of the Ambattur railway station for express and fast local trains towards Chennai to halt; they demand a government hospital as people have to go to Kilpauk Medical College Hospital or Stanley Hospital in case of emergencies

title: Interview with Anirudhya Mitra, author of The Delhi Directive, a spy thriller
content: Mitra says he uses fiction to show how complex and disciplined Indian intelligence operations really are

title: Reiterated demand for Hasina extradition with India: Bangladesh Foreign Minister Rahman
content: Bangladesh FM Khalilur Rahman says more meetings ahead of PM Tarique Rahman’s visit, outlooks regional cooperation amidst West Asia crisis

title: Karnataka’s Gruha Jyothi scheme dimmed the scope of PM’s Surya Ghar Muft Bijli Yojana: KRESMA
content: ‘Karnataka has one of the lowest adoptions with some 23,900 households under Surya Ghar Yojana while Gujarat reported over 5.7 lakh homes under rooftop solar systems installations’

title: President Murmu, PM Modi, leaders pay tribute to Mahatma Jyotirao Phule on 200th birth anniversary
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi, describing Phule as a great reformer, said his life was one of moral courage, restless enquiry and unshakable commitment to social good

title: Teams led by CSIR women scientists report advances in research on depression mechanisms in females
content: A group of scientists at Hyderabad’s CSIR-IICT are leading transformative research in decoding female-specific depression mechanisms. Their work not only addresses critical scientific gaps but also advances inclusive healthcare solutions, highlighting the growing impact of women in India’s research ecosystem

title: Bengaluru man saved after posting ‘last day’ status on WhatsApp; police act in four minutes after being alerted on Namma 112 helpline
content: A friend who saw the status immediately alerted the Namma 112 Emergency Helpline; police found the 28-year-old man lying unconscious on the floor.

title: Kerala Assembly polls: Further updates on voter turnout awaited
content: The ECINET app has been displaying 78.27% as the voter turnout since Thursday night. This is ‘provisional’ and does not include service voters and postal ballots

title: From a DMK campaigner and fan of MGR to renowned littérateur
content: Kalapriya says that before joining the DMK, MGR was associated with the Congress. Over the time, he surrounded himself with seasoned political minds, including communist leaders K.  Baladhandayutham  and  Kalyanasundaram. He was further supported by experienced figures such as K.A. Krishnaswamy  and Panruti S. Ramachandran. This combination of cinematic influence and political acumen gave him an exceptional grasp of governance as well as mass psychology.

title: House-to-house search drive in Mangalhat leads to multiple seizures

title: Women's transfer rankings for 2026-27 season

title: What the DOJ's NFL investigation could mean for networks, fans
